channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Gandhi Has Been Portrayed Across Global Pop Culture
item.description: This article explores how Mahatma Gandhi, India's most iconic freedom fighter, has been represented across films, television, and international pop culture since Independence. It opens by noting how B...

item.title: Cuba Warns of Strong Resistance Amid Escalating U.S. Rhetoric and Sanctions
item.description: The article describes rising tensions between the United States and Cuba following statements attributed to former President Donald Trump suggesting the possibility of taking control of the island. Cu...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Holds Ongoing Hearings on Alleged Corruption and Political Interference
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry is continuing its proceedings on Friday, focusing on allegations of criminal conduct, corruption, and political meddling within South Africa's criminal justice syste...

item.title: Justice Carpio donates 1875 map that defines Philippine sovereignty in West Philippine Sea
item.description: Former Supreme Court Senior Associate Justice Antonio Carpio has unveiled and donated to the National Library of the Philippines a rare 1875 map titled 'Carta General del Archipielago Filipino' that h...

item.title: Rising Antisemitic Violence Targets Jewish Communities Far from Israel
item.description: The article by Jonathan Freedland discusses a disturbing surge in antisemitic attacks across Western countries, noting that these assaults are wrongly framed by some as acts of opposition to the Israe...

item.title: Escalation in Iran-Israel conflict continues as U.S. deploys forces and global tensions rise
item.description: The ongoing Iran-Israel conflict has intensified further, with the United States increasing its military presence in West Asia and diplomatic tensions rising across multiple regions. Despite earlier i...

item.title: Dar es Salaam exhibition to celebrate young creatives ahead of Sanaa Blast Festival’s 10th anniversary
item.description: Dar es Salaam will soon host a major showcase of artistic creativity as the Creative Arts Exhibition takes place on April 11, 2026, at the Kijitonyama Postal Grounds. The event, coordinated by Benison...

item.title: Feyisara Bolarinwa-Adebowale named Ogun Bureau of Public Procurement Director-General after merit-based selection
item.description: The Ogun State Government don announce say dem don appoint Feyisara Bolarinwa-Adebowale as the new Director-General of the Bureau of Public Procurement. The State Head of Service, Kehinde Onasanya, ma...

item.title: Tanzania Looks to Durban Port for Container Handling Improvement
item.description: The Tanazanian government, under the leadership of Transport Deputy Minister David Kihenzile, has been keenly observing the operations of the Port of Durban, South Africa, to learn from its successes ...

item.title: Trump Enjoys White House Renovations Despite Melania's Disapproval
item.description: President Donald Trump has expressed delight at the ongoing construction at the White House, particularly the development of a new 22,000-square-foot ballroom, despite the first lady, Melania Trump, r...

item.title: Jury Finds Musk Liable for Misleading Twitter Investors During 2022 Buyout Dispute
item.description: A California civil jury has determined that Elon Musk intentionally misled Twitter investors during his attempt to withdraw from his $44 billion acquisition of the company in 2022. The case stems from...

item.title: Blind GBV survivor overcomes trauma to study and rebuild her independence
item.description: At just 20 years old, Zandiswa Ntshamba has shown extraordinary resilience after surviving a horrific gender-based violence (GBV) attack that left her blind. She was only 16 when she was attacked in e...

item.title: DCI Clarifies Harambee House Fraud Was Conducted by External Con Artists, Not Government Officials
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has dismissed reports linking government officials to a major fraud case at Harambee House in Nairobi. In a statement, DCI Director John Marete said th...

item.title: Technical Details of China's Lanyue Lunar Module and Its Landing Process
item.description: The Chinese lunar module Lanyue (揽月), weighing 26 tons, represents the cornerstone of China’s first crewed lunar landing mission, expected within four years. Designed to work with the Mengzhou-Y space...

item.title: Tanzanian researcher develops low-cost liquid organic fertiliser for smallholder farmers
item.description: This news highlights the important role of research in solving real-life challenges, especially in agriculture. It focuses on the work of Christina Hambo, a PhD student in Agroecology at Sokoine Unive...

item.title: Premier Danielle Smith says she has not been approached by RCMP in Alberta government contract investigation
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith stated on Thursday that she has not been contacted by the RCMP, despite the ongoing police searches related to an investigation into a provincial contract. The probe rep...

item.title: Matthew Dellavedova's Leadership Drives Sydney Kings to NBL Grand Final
item.description: Matthew Dellavedova has been pivotal in transforming the Sydney Kings into championship contenders in the 2025-26 NBL season. Despite being 35, Dellavedova's intensity, leadership, and commitment to t...

item.title: President Samia Provides Food and Basic Supplies to Vulnerable Families in Dar es Salaam for Eid Celebrations
item.description: Rais Samia Suluhu Hassan ametoa msaada wa chakula na mahitaji muhimu kwa familia zenye uhitaji katika wilaya tano za Dar es Salaam kama sehemu ya maandalizi ya sikukuu ya Eid al-Fitr. Kwa mujibu wa Mk...

item.title: University of the Free State investigates lecturer over allegedly offensive remark to students
item.description: The University of the Free State (UFS) has suspended a lecturer following complaints from students that he referred to them as 'monkeys' during a lecture on March 18, 2026, at the Bloemfontein campus....

item.title: Tanzania Highlights Major Progress in Power Generation and Renewable Energy at Bharat Electricity Summit
item.description: At the Bharat Electricity Summit 2026 held in New Delhi, Tanzania’s Permanent Secretary for Electricity and Renewable Energy, Engineer Felchesmi Mramba, presented the significant progress the country ...
